text.title: Dark Knowledge Extraction Protocol Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Dark Knowledge Extraction Protocol application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Protocol MUST run exactly 5 themed 90-minute sessions: architecture, hot files & landmines, on-call history, key contacts & politics, AI-agent rules. Ad-hoc 'just tell me what you know' sessions are rejected as low-yield.
rule.rationale: Themed sessions force the leaver to recall in different modes (spatial, narrative, social); empirical comparison vs unstructured shows 2-3x more captured items per hour.

rule.statement: At least one session MUST be a screen-shared repo-walk where the interviewer asks the leaver to open files cold and narrate. Pure-conversation interviews are rejected.
rule.rationale: Tacit knowledge surfaces in front of the actual artefact; abstract recall under-samples landmines that the leaver only remembers when they see the line of code.

rule.statement: Interviewer and scribe MUST be different people. Same person doing both produces sanitised, summary-shaped notes that lose the texture.
rule.rationale: Cognitive load of asking + listening + writing simultaneously degrades each output; separating roles is standard interview-research practice.

rule.statement: Receiving engineer MUST execute 3 representative tasks against the pack within 14 days of handover; gaps found are fed back as a v1.1 patch.
rule.rationale: Hand-over packs that are never used decay invisibly; the validation tasks force the pack to face reality before the leaver loses context.

rule.statement: Pack MUST include a section 'do not let the AI agent do X here' with concrete file globs + reasons. Generic 'AI agents must be careful' notes are rejected.
rule.rationale: AI agents now write a meaningful share of the code; a hand-over that skips this section creates immediate post-rotation incidents.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
